metadata.title: Guideline on the clinical and non clinical evaluation during the consultation procedure on medicinal substances contained in drug-eluting (medicinal substance-eluting) coronary stents

rules.rule_text: Bench testing must demonstrate that the ancillary medicinal substance and device neither chemically nor physically interact adversely with each other. Fatigue, corrosion, coating integrity, and durability must also be evaluated.
rules.verbatim_quote: It must be demonstrated that the ancillary medicinal substance and device neither chemically nor physically interact adversely with each other. In addition, it is important for the Manufacturer to elucidate how application of the medicinal substance and drug-carrier to the device may affect its fatigue and corrosion properties, coating integrity, durability, and any other relevant combination- product specific components.

rules.rule_text: Biocompatibility testing of the bare stent platform must be submitted, conforming to ISO 10993. Results for all relevant materials including carrier and stent material must be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Manufacturer must submit results of biocompatibility testing of the bare stent platform performed to support the initiation of a human clinical study as described in the Essential Requirements. The results of biocompatibility testing of all relevant materials including carrier and stent material shall be provided to the NCA/EMEA. The Manufacturer should document and discuss the extent of biocompatibility testing conform ISO 10993.

rules.rule_text: The manufacturer must elucidate the mechanism of action of the added medicinal product, justified by relevant data including cell culture studies examining inhibition of cell proliferation.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Manufacturer should elucidate the mechanism of action of the added medicinal product(s) justified by relevant data. Several biological effects, such as inhibition of cell proliferation, can be examined in cell culture utilising vascular endothelial and smooth cells.

rules.rule_text: In vivo pharmacokinetic studies are very important to quantify the duration of medicinal substance exposure. Concentrations must be measured at local (tissue), regional (organ), and systemic levels in animals.
rules.verbatim_quote: In vivo pharmacokinetic studies are very important to quantify the duration of medicinal substance exposure (B, C, D). Ancillary medicinal substance concentrations should be measured at the local (tissue), regional (organ), and systemic levels in animals (B, C, D).
rules.exceptions: For very small doses, time-release profiles may suffice for human trials.

rules.rule_text: The effect of stent overlap on vascular healing must be evaluated in the context of multiple overlapping stents.
rules.verbatim_quote: Within the context of clinical practice of implanting multiple overlapping stents, the effect of stent overlap on vascular healing should be evaluated.

rules.rule_text: Preclinical dose range finding studies are strongly recommended, showing effects across ranges from sub-therapeutic to toxic levels. A multiple dose study must be performed in an animal model to establish safety margins.
rules.verbatim_quote: Preclinical dose range finding studies are strongly recommended, showing effects across ranges from sub-therapeutic to toxic levels, where practicable (B, C, D). A multiple dose study should be performed in an animal model to establish safety margins, and toxicity in choosing a dose for clinical trials (B, C, D).

rules.rule_text: Stent efficacy in preclinical studies must be assessed by an absence of thrombosis and by neointimal reduction. Data at an early time point (3 or 7 days), at 28 days, and at least one late time point (3 or 6 months) are required.
rules.verbatim_quote: Stent efficacy should be assessed by an absence of thrombosis and by neointimal reduction. Data obtained at an early time point (3 or 7 days) should help determine subacute thrombosis risk. Other time points used should be at 28 days to observe neointimal hyperplasia, and at least one late time point to examine long term effects.

rules.rule_text: All animals experiencing death or other untoward clinical events must be examined. Sudden death later than 24 hours must be vigorously investigated for cause.
rules.verbatim_quote: All animals experiencing death or other untoward clinical events should be examined. Such deaths typically occur in the first 24 hours after implant, but may occur later if healing is impaired. Sudden death later than 24 hours should be vigorously investigated for cause, as the ancillary medicinal substance might have interfered with healing.

rules.rule_text: Simple visual description of histopathology is discouraged as the sole evaluation. A rigorous semi-quantitative and defined scale for arteriography and histopathology evaluation must be used.
rules.verbatim_quote: Simple visual description of the histopathology is discouraged as the sole evaluation. A more rigorous (semi) quantitative and defined scale for arteriography and histopathology evaluation (inflammation, vascular healing, endothelization) should be presented as well.

rules.rule_text: When a carrier is present, additional controls to evaluate the carrier alone (without the ancillary medicinal substance) must be included.
rules.verbatim_quote: When a carrier is present, additional controls to evaluate the carrier alone, without the ancillary medicinal substance, must be included.

rules.rule_text: If the medicinal substance is a new active substance (Category D), human Phase I studies in healthy volunteers are required to determine the no observed adverse effect level (NOEL).
rules.verbatim_quote: In case of "D", human Phase I studies are to be expected. An additional requirement would be an initial human testing in healthy volunteers intended to determine the no observed adverse effect level. ... Human toxicity Phase I studies are to be expected to determine the no observed adverse effect level (NOEL) if the medicinal substance is not approved (D).

rules.rule_text: The usual standard of evidence for a DES is a randomised, controlled clinical trial. Historical control is rarely acceptable for a novel stent.
rules.verbatim_quote: The usual standard of evidence for a DES is the randomised, controlled clinical trial. A historical control is rarely acceptable for a novel stent.

rules.rule_text: Actively controlled studies with a commercially available DES are expected. The choice of control group must be critically evaluated and justified to ascertain best-of-care approaches.
rules.verbatim_quote: Actively controlled studies where patients are treated with a commercially available DES are expected. Choices of control groups for these trials should be critically evaluated and justified in order to ascertain that best of care approaches are being used.

rules.rule_text: A non-inferiority design with an already approved DES is only acceptable if patients are included according to approved conditions of use (indications). Background therapy must be standardised according to available recommendations.
rules.verbatim_quote: A non-inferiority design with an already approved DES would only be acceptable if patients are included according to the approved conditions of use (indications). In all cases, background therapy should be standardised according to available recommendations.

rules.rule_text: Specific evidence for usefulness and safety across complex lesions or patient cohorts (small vessels, long lesions, bifurcations, multivessel disease, diabetes, coronary grafts) will be requested.
rules.verbatim_quote: Specific evidence for the usefulness and safety of the DES across more complex lesions or patients cohorts (i.e. small vessels, long lesions, lesions at bifurcations, multi vessel disease, diabetes, coronary grafts) will be requested.

rules.rule_text: For novel DES settings (categories B, C, D), clinically meaningful endpoints are strongly recommended: ischemia-driven revascularisation of the target lesion, cardiac death, and myocardial infarction should be used as the primary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: In terms of study endpoints for other settings of coronary drug-eluting stents (B, C, D), clinically meaningful endpoints are strongly recommended. Ischemia-driven revascularisation of the target lesion, cardiac death and myocardial infarction, should be used as primary endpoint.
rules.exceptions: For category A2-4 with comparable release characteristics, angiographic/IVUS biomarkers may be acceptable as primary endpoints.

rules.rule_text: A significant improvement of an angiographic/intravascular ultrasound parameter does not necessarily translate into a better clinical outcome. Clinical outcome conclusions from such studies may be underpowered.
rules.verbatim_quote: a significant improvement of an angiographic/intravascular ultrasound parameter does not necessarily translate into a better clinical outcome. Conclusions regarding possible improvements of clinical outcome could be difficult since the study will be underpowered.

rules.rule_text: Individual components of composite endpoints and clinically relevant groups of components must be analysed separately to show consistent results.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended to analyse in addition the single components and clinically relevant groups of components separately, to show consistent results.

rules.rule_text: Death and MI rates must be reported along with sensitivity analysis based on accepted thresholds. Abrupt stent occlusion (ARC definitions), bleeding including stroke must also be reported.
rules.verbatim_quote: Death and myocardial infarction (rates to be reported along with sensitivity analysis based on accepted thresholds), abrupt stent occlusion (ARC definitions), bleeding including stroke, should be reported in order to capture complications of anti-platelet therapy.

rules.rule_text: Anti-platelet regimens (concomitant therapies) must be specified and justified. Potential interference of concomitant therapy on study endpoints must be specifically considered.
rules.verbatim_quote: Specific consideration should be given to the potential interference/contribution of concomitant therapy on study endpoints. Concomitant therapies; e.g. anti-platelet regimes, should be specified and justified.

rules.rule_text: Pivotal clinical studies providing data on primary endpoints must last at least one year with careful follow-up. Additional angiographic evaluation at 12-24 months could provide information on longitudinal healing response.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, pivotal clinical studies providing data on primary endpoints should last at least one year with careful follow-up. ... additional angiographic evaluation at 12 to 24 months could provide additional information on the longitudinal healing response.

rules.rule_text: Long-term post-approval clinical follow-up (e.g., 5-year) must be carried out to determine issues of long-term safety and performance. Data must feed into the risk management process.
rules.verbatim_quote: Long-term post approval clinical follow up (e.g. 5-year) should be carried out in order to determine issues of long term safety and performance according to the requirements of MEDDEV 2.12-2. Data from this follow-up should be fed into the risk management process.
